---
title: Kafka Overview
description: Overview of the Apache Kafka templates — KRaft clusters and web UI.
---

import Mermaid from '../../../../components/Mermaid.astro';

This section covers three Kafka-related templates that work together:

| Template | Purpose | When to use |
|---|---|---|
| [3-node cluster with auth](/templates/kafka/cluster-with-auth/) | Production KRaft cluster with SASL/PLAIN | Team or production environments |
| [3-node cluster without auth](/templates/kafka/cluster-without-auth/) | KRaft cluster, no authentication | Local development only |
| [Kafka UI](/templates/kafka/kafka-ui/) | Web interface for managing clusters | Alongside any cluster |

## KRaft Architecture

Both cluster templates use **KRaft mode** — Apache Kafka's built-in Raft consensus protocol that replaces ZooKeeper. Each of the 3 brokers also acts as a KRaft controller.

<Mermaid diagram={`
graph TB
    subgraph KRaft["3-node KRaft Cluster"]
        subgraph B1["Broker 1 (node.id=1)"]
            roles1["roles: broker + controller"]
            ext1[":9092 external"]
            ctrl1[":9093 controller"]
            int1[":29092 internal"]
        end
        subgraph B2["Broker 2 (node.id=2)"]
            roles2["roles: broker + controller"]
            ext2[":9094 external"]
            ctrl2[":9095 controller"]
            int2[":29093 internal"]
        end
        subgraph B3["Broker 3 (node.id=3)"]
            roles3["roles: broker + controller"]
            ext3[":9096 external"]
            ctrl3[":9097 controller"]
            int3[":29094 internal"]
        end
    end

    ctrl1 <-->|"Raft quorum"| ctrl2
    ctrl2 <-->|"Raft quorum"| ctrl3
    ctrl1 <-->|"Raft quorum"| ctrl3

    ExternalClient["External client"] -->|"KAFKA_HOST_IP:9092/9094/9096"| KRaft
    KafkaUI["Kafka UI\n(kafka-ui-net)"] -->|"kafka1:29092\nkafka2:29093\nkafka3:29094"| KRaft
`} />

## Shared Network

Both cluster templates join `kafka-ui-net` so that Kafka UI can reach brokers by container name. This network must exist before starting either template:

```bash
docker network create kafka-ui-net
```

## Required Configuration for All Clusters

Before starting any Kafka cluster template, you must set:

**`KAFKA_CLUSTER_ID`** — A unique UUID for this KRaft cluster. Changing this after the cluster has data will break the cluster.
```bash
docker run --rm confluentinc/cp-kafka:7.8.0 kafka-storage random-uuid
```

**`KAFKA_HOST_IP`** — The IP address of the Docker host, used for external listener advertisement. External Kafka clients connect to this IP.

```bash
# Linux
ip route get 1 | awk '{print $7; exit}'

# macOS
ipconfig getifaddr en0

# Docker Desktop (Mac or Windows)
# Use the value: host.docker.internal
```

See [Prerequisites](/prerequisites/#kafka-finding-your-host-ip) for details.

## Listener Types

| Listener name | Used by | Address |
|---|---|---|
| `EXTERNAL` / `SASL_EXTERNAL` | External clients (producers/consumers) | `KAFKA_HOST_IP:9092/9094/9096` |
| `INTERNAL` / `PLAINTEXT_INTERNAL` | Broker-to-broker replication | `kafka1/2/3:29092/29093/29094` |
| `CONTROLLER` | KRaft controller quorum | `kafka1/2/3:9093/9095/9097` |

## Template Selection Guide

**Use the auth cluster when:**
- Multiple people or applications will connect to the cluster
- The host is accessible beyond your personal machine
- You're building towards a production deployment

**Use the no-auth cluster when:**
- You're prototyping or learning Kafka locally
- Only your own applications connect to the cluster
- The host is not accessible from outside your machine

**Always add Kafka UI when:**
- You want to browse topics, consumer groups, and messages visually
- You want to create topics without using the CLI

## Starting a Full Kafka Stack

```bash
# 1. Create the shared network
docker network create kafka-ui-net

# 2. Start the cluster (choose one)
cd kafka/confluent/3-node-cluster-with-auth
# OR
cd kafka/confluent/3-node-cluster-without-auth

cp .env.example .env
# Edit .env: set KAFKA_CLUSTER_ID, KAFKA_HOST_IP, and passwords (auth only)
docker compose up -d

# 3. Start the UI
cd kafka/confluent/kafka-ui && docker compose up -d

# 4. Open Kafka UI
open http://localhost:8080
# Settings → Configure new cluster
# Bootstrap servers: kafka1:29092,kafka2:29093,kafka3:29094
```
